steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: "At least 35" means x $\geq$ 35; "no more than 60" means x $\leq$ 60.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Both constraints must hold simultaneously, so combine them into one compound inequality.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: The compound inequality is $35 \leq x \leq 60$, which means x is between 35 and 60 inclusive.

choices.elimination:
• This captures 'at least 35' but places no upper bound on speed.
  • The bus could travel at 200 mph under this inequality, which violates the 'no more than 60' condition.
  • Eliminated because the upper bound is missing.

• This would mean the bus travels at 60 mph or more, which misses the lower bound of 35.
  • It also excludes speeds between 35 and 59, which are permitted by the problem.
  • Eliminated because it misrepresents both constraints.

• 'At least 35' gives $x \geq 35$; 'no more than 60' gives $x \leq 60$.
  • Writing both together yields $35 \leq x \leq 60$, which is exactly this choice.
  • That is the correct answer.

• 95 = 35 + 60, but summing the two speed limits is not a valid mathematical operation for expressing this constraint.
  • This inequality allows speeds as low as 0 and as high as 95, neither bound correctly reflecting the problem.
  • Eliminated because it doesn't represent either the lower or upper bound correctly.
